title: viola rupestris var. glaberrima
intro: Viola rupestris var. glaberrima, also known as the Glaberrime Rock Violet or the Glaberrime Violet, is a variety of the Viola rupestris plant, a species of perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the violet family (Violaceae). It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The plant is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as skin conditions, like wounds and eczema, due to its soothing and protective properties. Herbal preparations of Viola rupestris var. glaberrima include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to make teas, topical creams, and ointments for various health benefits.
